secondary_archetype: Chief of Staff / mini-COO for Pet insurance line

level_signals: 6+ years in management consulting or business operations with leadership experience
'mini-COO' framing implies significant autonomy and ownership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years in management consulting (strategy and operations) or business operations with leadership experience
Track record of driving impact and influencing decisions across multiple stakeholder groups
Ability to adapt quickly in a fast-moving environment while maintaining strategic focus
Strong analytical mindset with experience turning data into business strategy
Experience leading cross-functional projects and building consensus across diverse teams
Ready to work in an office environment most days of the week

scope.surface: Pet insurance line — profitability and growth strategy; cross-functional strategic initiatives
core_responsibilities: Drive profitability and growth strategies for the Pet insurance business as a strategic business partner
Lead cross-functional initiatives spanning Product, Insurance, Finance, and Customer Operations teams
Take strategic projects from concept to execution across multiple stakeholder groups
Analyze complex business challenges and translate insights into actionable recommendations
Influence decision-making at the leadership level while building alignment across diverse teams
Navigate rapidly changing priorities and guide teams through strategic pivots
Partner with Product, Actuarial, Finance, and Revenue teams to optimize business performance

green_flags: Clear product ownership framing ('mini-COO' for Pet line) signals real scope and autonomy
AI-first company culture explicitly stated as operational, not just marketing
Cross-functional scope spans multiple high-impact teams (Product, Actuarial, Finance, Revenue)
red_flags: No specific city listed for an in-office role — location of office is unclear
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
No mention of people management yet 'leadership experience' is required — scope of leadership is ambiguous

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior Lead', years requirement, and 'mini-COO' framing)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; 'leadership experience' mentioned but no direct reports stated)
scope.business_model (inferred from product description — B2C insurance, AI-first)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
location.type (inferred as onsite from 'office environment most days of the week')
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false from onsite requirement)
ambiguities: No office city or country is specified in the JD body. Lemonade operates in the US and Israel; the role location is ambiguous.

'Leadership experience' is required but it is unclear whether this means managing direct reports or leading projects/initiatives — treated as project leadership given no direct mention of people management.
